title: Miconia penninervis
taxonomy: Miconia penninervis is classified within the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family consists of approximately 170 genera and around 5,000 species. Miconia penninervis is a member of the genus Miconia, a large genus of flowering plants with around 1,000 species. The genus Miconia is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Miconia penninervis is specifically native to the tropical regions of Central America.

intro: Miconia penninervis is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the Miconia tree. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. Bioactive constituents of Miconia penninervis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Miconia penninervis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and other health concerns.
